title: The One With The Tiny T-shirt

lines.text: Central Perk, Gunther is pouring Rachel coffee.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: (In his head) Say Rachel, I was wondering if you'd like to go to a movie with me sometime. As my lover! Nnnsch, to out there. Maybe you'd just like to ah, get something to eat with me sometime? As my lover.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: (entering) Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! All right, let's go shoppin'!!

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Um, y'know, before we go ah, there's something I need to say.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, okay.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: I've kinda of had this ah, this crush on you. (Rachel is shocked) But since you were with Ross, I-I didn't do anything about it. But, now that you're not, I'd really like to ask you out sometime. So-so that's-that's what I'm doing, now.
Gunther gets this hurt expression on his face and goes into the back room

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! Umm....
She's interrupted by a loud crash and the sound of braking dishes. Followed quickly by another crash. Everyone turns and looks at the back room, as Gunther emerges.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: I dropped a cup.
Opening Credits
The hallway between the two apartments, Pete and Monica are returning from their date.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: ...so y'know, that's why, within a few years, that voice recognition is gonna be pretty much standard on any computer you buy. Y'know, so you could be like-like, 'Wash my car.' 'Clean my room.' It's not gonna be able to do any of those things, but it'll understand what you're saying.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, this is so great.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Yeah, it was.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right then. (He leans in to kiss her goodnight, but she quickly kisses him on the cheek and pats his shoulder.) Bye.
She goes into her apartment and sees Chandler, Phoebe, and Ross sitting there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hello, people who do not live here.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hi! Hello!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I gave you a key for emergencies!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We were out of Doritos.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, how'd the date go with Mr. Millionaire?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Mr. Millionaire, new from Snooty Playthings! Third wife sold separately.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He's great! I mean we have such a good time together! He's so funny, and sooo sweet, and I'm not attracted to him at all!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Still?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Noo!! It's driving me crazy. I mean every other way he's like the perfect guy, he has everything. Plus! He actually has everything.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Life-sized Imperial Storm Troopers from Sharper Image?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Two.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow!! Can Joey and I put them on and fight?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering, dancing and singing) Oh mommie, oh daddie, I am a big old baddie! Oh mommie, oh daddie, I am a big old baddie! (He dances around the dinner table and exits)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I guess he musta gotten the part in that play.

lines.speaker: Monica and Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, either that, or Gloria Estefan was right, eventually, the rhythm is going to get you.
A Theatre, Joey is arriving to rehearse the play he's in.

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: Joe. How's it going?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Good. (He sets his stuff down and starts talking to Kate, another cast member.) Hey.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, so you're playing Adrienne, huh?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Yes. Are you one of the retarded cousins?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, no. Ah, I playing your husband, Victor. I'm Joey Tribianni.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Hi, nice to meet you. Kate Miller.
She goes over to the snack table, and Joey quickly runs over and pours her a cup of coffee.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So the ah, play's pretty great, huh?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Oh, yeah. I love Jennifer Van Murray's work. She's so brilliantly incisive when it comes to deconstructing the psyche of the American middle class.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, forget about it. She rocks!

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Where do I know you from?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dr. Drake Remoray. Days of Our Lives. Voted most datable neurosurgeon by Teen Beat.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: No, that's not it. So, you're a soap actor? Well this must be pretty exciting for you to be in a real play, hmm?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, I've done plays before. I'm a serious actor.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: That info-mercial! For the milk carton spout thing! You're-you're-you're the guy that doesn't know how to pour milk!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: See, I actually can pour milk, but I got you believing that I couldn't. Now, see, that's acting.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Right, at the end, you choked on a cookie.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, that was real.
Central Perk, Rachel is telling Phoebe about Mark, as Gunther eavesdrops behind them.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! I cannot believe Mark asked you out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What, so what are you gonna tell him?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I told him I would think about it, but I'm gonna tell him no.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Huh.
Gunther tries to swoop in to ask Rachel out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I mean I think I'd say no to anybody right now. (Hearing this Gunther swoops back to cleaning tables.) Oh, but it was so strange. I mean I'm standing there with this charming, cute guy, who's asking me to go out with him, which I'm allowed to do, and I felt guilty. Y'know, like I'd be cheating on Ross or something.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow. So, okay, maybe that means that, you're not over Ross yet and you have issues with your father.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't have any issues with my Father.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, so it's probably just the Ross thing then.
Chandler and Joey's, Joey is telling Chandler about Kate.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I hate this woman!! I hate her! She told everyone in the company about that info-mercial, and now they all keep asking me to open their drinks. Okay, and whenever I can't do it, they're all like-like laughing at me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hello.

lines.speaker: Chandler and Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So Rachel called. Wants to see me. Going over in a minute.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow, what-what do you think she wants?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, maybe the crazy fog has lifted and she realises that life without me.... a-sucks.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's possible. You are very loveable, I'd miss you if I broke up with you. (Ross glares at him) I was just trying to be supportive.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Then be supportive like a guy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (in a deep voice) If I broke up with you, I'd miss you.
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel is there as Ross enters, walking very confidently.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You ah, wanted to see me?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. Ahh, here's a box of your stuff. (hands him a box)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, y'know, it's just like hats, and a shirt, and CD's, just sort of stuff that you've left here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What are you doing? Are you trying to hurt me? Or something?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. Ross, it, it just seems that y'know it's time we-we y'know, move on. I mean, I mean don't' you think?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, I do.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Good.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I-I really do. (takes a dinosaur mug out of the box) Hey! This-this was a gift?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, you got that for free from the museum gift shop.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's still a gift! I got it from the gift shop!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, all right, give me the mug! I'll keep the mug.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No!! Y'know-y'know don't do me any favours. In fact, where, where's the rest of my stuff?! Huh? Like-like my umm, (picks up a book) Hey, this book is mine!! And-and-and, and that T-shirt you sleep in? I'd like that back too. Yes, I do.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You know how much I love that T-shirt! You never even where that T-shirt!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm just trying to help you, move on.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, you are a petty man. You are a petty, petty....

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Petty...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Petty... (goes into her room)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Petty...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Petty...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Petty...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Small...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Small... (Rachel comes back into the living room and catches Ross mocking her.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You are so just doing this out of spite.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Awwwahuh, no, no, no!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm-I'm gonna wear this all the time! I love this shirt!! (he kisses the shirt)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You have not worn that T-shirt since you were 15!! It doesn't even fit you anymore!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, yeah-yeah-yeah-yeah...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (imitating him) yeah-yeah-yeah!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh-oh, okay, okay! (He quickly takes off his sweater in order to put on the T-shirt. It's an old 'Frankie says relax' T-shirt, that barely fits him. Rachel nods her head in approval of the new look.) If you don't mind I'm gonna the rest of my stuff, and relax, in my favourite shirt. (Starts to leave) You have a pleasant evening. (He exits and leaves the door open.)
Central Perk, the gang minus Rachel and Ross are talking to Pete.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, you're like a zillionaire? (Pete smiles and nods)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And you're our age. You're our age.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know what, you should like, you should buy a state and then just name it after yourself.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: What like Pete Dakota?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, or, or, or, Mississ-Pete.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, oh, I got it! Pete-Chicago.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's not a state Joe.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, and Mississ-Pete is?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: I got to go, so ah, I'll see you guys later.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're our age!

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: (to Monica, by the door) So ah, we on for tomorrow?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Absolutely! (He goes to kiss her, but she stops him and rubs his head and says...) Now get out of here you!! (Pete leaves) (to the gang) Okay, I'm running out of places I can touch him! Look, is there something wrong with me? I mean why am I only attracted to guys where there's no future? Either they're too old, or they're too young, and then there's Pete who's-who's crazy about me, and who's absolutely perfect for me, and there's like zip going on! I mean, seriously, does it sound like something's wrong with me?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, kinda.
The Theatre, Joey and Kate are rehearsing for the play.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Happy?! Is that what I'm supposed to be Vic? Happy?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, why don't you tell me what you're supposed to be! Huh? Because I sure as hell can't figure it out! I talk to you and nothin'. You look at me, and it's nothin'. (He kisses her) Nothing.

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: Tasty! I'm really starting to feel like you guys have a history, it's-it's nice.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: I have a question about this scene.

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Well, I don't understand why Adrienne's attracted to Victor.

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: Peel the onion. First of all, he's good looking.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: I think my character's gonna need a little bit more of reason than that.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, hey, how about this one. Ah, it's says so in the script! Y'know ah, I-I don't know why my character likes you either, I mean it says in the script here that you're a bitch.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: It doesn't say that in the script.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It does in mine!
The Hallway, Chandler and Ross are returning from working out.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can blow dry it. I can put gel on it. It doesn't matter, I still wind up with this little (pats the flat spot on the back of his head) cowlicky thing on the middle part of my head. It's so annoying. Does it bug you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You bug me.
[Rachel comes out of her apartment, followed by Mark, and they leave on their date, without saying a word to Ross. Ross is stunned.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Is there any chance you didn't see that?
Commercial Break
Chandler and Joey's, Chandler is watching a basketball game, Ross is staring out the peephole.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ross! You gotta stop! Okay?! You can't just stare through the peep hole for three hours! You're gonna get peep eye!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I knew it! I knew it! I always knew she liked him! Y'know, she'd say no, but here we are! Right? We just broke up, first thing she does!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You didn't just break up.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, it's been like three weeks!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You slept with somebody three hours after you thought you broke up. I mean bullets have left guns slower!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Here they come, here they come. Oh-ho, if she kisses him goodnight, I'm gonna kill myself, I swear. I can't, I can't watch this. (turns away, then quickly turns to look again) Come on, date over! Date over! Uh-oh, here we go, she's going in.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: She's going in. Wait! He's going in! He's going in!! The door's closed! I, I can't see anything but the door closed!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And the inventor of the door rests happily in his grave.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, I have to do something. I mean, I have, I have to stop it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Stop what?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't know, but I ah, I have the feeling that my being there will do it. I'll go over and I will borrow something. Juice!! I need juice!!
He starts to exit, but Chandler tries to stop him by climbing on his back and grabbing hold of the foosball table.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No!! You can't!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, they must be stopped!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I am your friend, and I am not gonna let you do this!! (Ross is now dragging Chandler and the foosball table to the door) You are surprisingly strong!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I need juice! People need juice!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look man!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: People need juice!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Listen to me!! (Chandler turns him around and closes and holds the door shut with his feet.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Juice, I need...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: She's moving on! Okay, if it's not this guy, it's gonna be somebody else! And unless you're thinking about subletting my peep hole, you are going to have to get used to the fact that the relationship is over! Okay, man? It's over.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's just I miss her so much.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know. (He rubs Ross's head)
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel is pouring Mark some coffee.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Why do all you're coffee mugs have numbers on the bottom?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh. That's so Monica can keep track. That way if one on them is missing, she can be like, 'Where's number 27?!'
She sits down, and Mark leans over and kisses her. Rachel doesn't react. He tries it again, and Rachel jumps back quickly.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know what?

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: No. And I don't think I'm gonna want to.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can't do this.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Yep. Yep, that's what I didn't want to know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, oh, Mark, I'm doing this for the wrong reasons, y'know? I'm just doing it to get back at Ross. I'm sorry, it's not very fair to you.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Ahh! Fair, schmair! Y'know? Look, if you want to get back at Ross, I am here for you. Really? No-no, I say-I say, I say we get back at him right on this couch. Right here!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God. I'm sorry about this.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: That's okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You sure?

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Yeah. I can just go home and get back at him by myself.
A Hospital Reception, Monica and Pete are there.

lines.speaker: Spokeswoman
lines.text: ...has become the penicillin of the twenty-first century. And so today, this hospital is about to take major steps toward leading that revolution. It is truly ironic, on one hand consider the size...

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: (to Monica) Hey, can I ask you something?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Where are we?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (looks around) Well, with all these doctors and nurses, I'm gonna say, midget rodeo.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Just tell me the truth.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. Umm, y'know, I don't think, I don't think I told you this, but umm, I just got out of a really serious relationship.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Oh, yeah. Wasn't that like a year ago?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So I did tell you. Okay, y'know, that really isn't the thing. Umm, the thing is that, right now I'm just in a place in my life where I need to focus on me. Y'know what I mean?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Oh, yeah. I know that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I so wanna be attracted to you.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: But you're not. Okay, good.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Y'know what, don't be. This is not, don't be, 'cause it's not so bad.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's not?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: I know I'm no John Bon Jovi, (Monica laughs) or someone who find attractive, I'm just, I think, y'know, that you might end up feeling differently.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, um, look I-I don't want this to come our wrong, but ah, you seem awfully confident for a guy I just told I wasn't attracted too.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Yeah, stupidly charming isn't' it? Well listen let's, you wanna get something to eat? 'Cause this place is kinda depressing. (they start to leave)

lines.speaker: Spokeswoman
lines.text: And the man who made all this possible... Mr. Peter Becker.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: (to Monica) One second.
He takes the scissors, cuts the ribbon, shakes her hand, posses for the picture, and leaves.
Chandler and Joey's, Chandler is sitting in one of the black chairs, and turns to face the other one.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't think this town is big enough for both of us to relax in. (He blows on his hand) Draw!! (He quickly pulls the lever to raise the foot rest, like a gunfighter in a Western.)
Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Joey) I wasn't doing anything. (Joey starts angrily throwing his stuff down.) Uh-oh, what did she do now?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Just because she went to Yale drama, she thinks she's like the greatest actress since, since, sliced bread!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ah, Sliced Bread, a wonderful Lady MacBeth.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: God, I just, I hate her! I hate her!! With her, 'Oh, I'm so talented.' and 'Oh, I'm so pretty,' and 'Ooh, I smell so good.'

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I think somebody has a crush on somebody.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, Chandler, can we please stay focused on my problem here?! Y'know?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm talking about you. You big, big freak.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh. (realises) Ohh. Ohh, you're out of your mind.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, you have nothing but talk about her for the last 48 hours! If you were in a school yard you'd be pulling her pigtails and pushing her down now!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, yeah?! Then how come I keep thinking about her in all these sexual scenarios and stuff huh?!
The Theatre, Kate and Joey are rehearsing the same scene as before.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Happy?! Is that what I'm supposed to be Vic? Happy?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, why don't you tell me what you're supposed to be! Huh? Because I sure as hell can't figure it out! I talk to you and it's nothin'. You look at me, and nothin'. (He kisses her, more passionately this time) Nothing.

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: You guys make me fly! High! Okay, we're gonna pick it up here, tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Well, that was ah...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Better?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Yeah! Yeah, it was definitely an improvement. G'night.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah, Kate?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You ah, you forgot your shoes.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: (she giggles) I'm probably gonna need those. Huh? (she giggles some more)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, listen you ah....

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Hmm?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: ...feel like getting a cup of coffee?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Umm.

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: (leaning in) Kate?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Yep.

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: You ready to go?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: (to Joey) Very nice. Very nice. (he walks away)

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: So umm, I'll see you tomorrow, huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, yeah sure, goodnight.
Monica and Rachel's, Monica is in the kitchen chopping vegetables. Chandler and Phoebe are sitting in the living room.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Chandler) Hey! (Chandler looks up, startled) Why isn't it Spiderman? Y'know like Goldman, Silverman...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: 'Cause it's-it's not his last name.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It isn't?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, it's not like, like Phil Spiderman. He's a spider, man. Y'know like ah, like Goldman is a last name, but there's no Gold Man.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, okay. There should be Gold Man!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, Rach, how was work?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, great. Although I did sit down where there wasn't a chair.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: By the way, Ross dropped by a box of your stuff.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, well, I guess I had that one coming. I'm just gonna throw it out, it's probably just a bunch of shampoo and... (she opens the box and stops)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Something wrong?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (She takes the T-shirt out of the box and holds it to her chest and take a deep breath.) No. Nothing. (She smiles and goes into her room.)
Closing Credits
Chandler and Joey's, Phoebe and Chandler are sitting in the black chairs.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, ahh, what kind of powers would Gold Man have?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay well, he would turn things to gold.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What about things that are already gold?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ahh, his work is done.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, let's play my game now.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. All right you yellow-bellied-lilly-livered-DRAW!! (they both kick up the foot rests like an old fashioned gun fight.)
End
